canonicalSpecs.purpose: App PC React package split, app SDK boundary, and desktop interaction rules.
Local component specs directory and manifest rules.
Runtime configuration, environment, SDK bootstrap, and feature flag rules.
Module README, examples, ADR, changelog, and runbook rules.
Canonical domain ownership and naming.
UI, service, SDK, accessibility, and frontend runtime rules.
Standard ownership, exception, compatibility, and migration rules.
User-facing language, locale, message catalog, and fallback rules.
Reusable package contract and dependency direction.
Latency, pagination, bundle, scalability, and retry budget rules.
Personal, tenant, sensitive, and regulated data rules.
SDKWork root standards entrypoint.
SDK generation and SDK integration rules.
Secure auth, token, secrets, CORS, validation, and logging rules.
Contract, frontend, SDK, security, parity, and documentation verification rules.
UI architecture selection, package-family ownership, and SDK boundary rules.
Authored source structure and generated code boundaries.
Canonical SDKWork naming rules.
TypeScript and Node package rules.
Frontend authored source structure.
Application shell and module composition rules.
SDKWork app manifest and release metadata rules.
SaaS, private, and local deployment parity rules.
PC browser, desktop, and tablet application root rules.

integration.authority: Root SDKWork specs remain authoritative. Local specs may extend but must not contradict them.
integration.dependencyPolicy: Consumers integrate through public package exports, declared runtime entrypoints, generated SDK clients, or documented adapters only.
integration.sdkPolicy: Generated SDK clients are injected through service/runtime boundaries; reusable UI must not create raw HTTP clients or manual auth headers.
integration.languagePolicy: Language-specific packages follow their native manifest and build tools while preserving the same component contract.
